text: A student whose CGPA falls below 2.0 at the end of a semester shall be placed on academic probation. A student on probation must raise their CGPA to at least 2.0 within the next two consecutive semesters, failing which the student shall be subject to dismissal from the program.
During the probation period, a student may not register for more than 12 credit hours per semester and must meet with their academic advisor at the start of each semester.
A minimum attendance of 75% in each course is required for a student to be eligible to sit in the final examination.
Re-checking of an examination paper may be requested within 15 days of result announcement by submitting Form R-1 along with the prescribed fee.
Plagiarism is classified into four levels based on the percentage of matched text: Level 1 (19-40%), Level 2 (40-60%), Level 3 (60-80%), and Level 4 (above 80%).
A Level 3 plagiarism finding against a faculty member shall result in a one-year debarment from supervising research and a fine equal to one basic monthly salary.
